stories.headline: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

stories.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants
stories.summary: US president’s warning marks latest attempts to assert American control over Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: U.S. Signs Deal to Help Saudis Obtain Nuclear Power
stories.summary: The accord is intended to further bind the United States with the Saudi government as the war with Iran strains the relationship.

stories.headline: US and Saudi Arabia announce nuclear cooperation deal
stories.summary: Trump administration says 'historic' agreement will 'reinforce global nonproliferation standards' and create jobs in US.

stories.headline: Iran warns of regional turmoil if US threats and attacks do not stop
stories.summary: Tehran's chief negotiator says if Iran's 'security is not ensured, no infrastructure will be safe'.

stories.headline: Trump threatens to bomb Tehran back for every Iranian attack in Hormuz
stories.summary: The US president said Washington would destroy one piece of civilian infrastructure — including sites near Tehran — each time Iran fires on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, as the war entered its 11th consecutive day of US strikes.

stories.headline: Iran warns Bulgaria over US aircraft deployment as US military 'stands ready to counter threats'
stories.summary: The warning follows a Bulgarian government proposal to temporarily deploy up to eight US aerial refuelling aircraft at Bezmer Air Base in the country’s southeast.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
stories.summary: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back.On Tuesday, Zelenskyy bowed to public demands by firing Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. He appointed Mykhailo Drapa

stories.headline: Key takeaways from Khalil al-Hayya’s first speech as Hamas leader
stories.summary: The newly appointed leader says Hamas would work to help the people of Gaza and achieve a united Palestinian state.

stories.headline: Hamas’s new leader calls for permanent Gaza ceasefire
stories.summary: In his first speech since being elected Hamas political chief, Khalil al-Hayya called for a permanent ceasefire in Gaza.

stories.headline: US and Saudi Arabia agree landmark nuclear energy pact
stories.summary: Washington says deal would pave the way for ‘decades-long, multibillion-dollar partnership’

stories.headline: Trump: Iran will ‘pay a big price’ for US troop deaths
stories.summary: US President Trump warned that Iran will ‘pay a big price’ right before he attended a dignified transfer ceremony.

stories.headline: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
stories.summary: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.headline: Wounded Gaza journalists call for urgent medical evacuation
stories.summary: Wounded and chronically ill Palestinian journalists held a protest calling for urgent medical evacuation from Gaza.

stories.headline: Drones hit Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second week running
stories.summary: Fires broke out at Wildberries warehouse sites in Krasnodar and Stavropol after overnight drone attacks, injuring 15 people and forcing both facilities to suspend operations.

stories.headline: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Isabel Rose was convicted of trying to extort £100,000 from the UK banker and perverting the course of justice.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones hit Russian online giant retailer Wildberries for second time
stories.summary: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight.

stories.headline: India's Modi government under growing pressure as 'cockroach' protests intensify
stories.summary: The CJP got a boost on Tuesday when the opposition Congress party demonstrated outside the PM's house.

stories.headline: Who is Mykhailo Drapatyi, Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
stories.summary: Drapatyi becomes Ukraine's third commander-in-chief since Russia's full-scale invasion in February 2022.

stories.headline: Ex-Australia batter David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving
stories.summary: Star cricketer was charged after failing a random breath test in Sydney earlier this year.

stories.headline: European gas prices approach Iran war highs as traders fret over winter supplies
stories.summary: Heatwaves and bidding war with Asian buyers leave market vulnerable to Strait of Hormuz disruption

stories.headline: Wall Street Won’t Give Up on Strategy, Sees 170% Upside
stories.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. is confronting a collapse of confidence almost everywhere — except among Wall Street analysts.

stories.headline: Venezuela Oil: Batistas’ Fluxus Clinches Stake in PDVSA Joint Venture
stories.summary: Brazil’s billionaire Batista brothers have acquired a stake in an oil production project in Venezuela, the latest deal to emerge in a country that the Trump administration is vowing to reinvigorate after years of sanctions.

stories.headline: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.

stories.headline: Arizona primary takeaways: Trump’s picks prevail and a House Democrat survives
stories.summary: Rep. Andy Biggs, who voted to overturn the 2020 election results, won the GOP nomination for governor. Among Democrats, a left-wing challenger failed to unseat Rep. Greg Stanton.

stories.headline: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British manA British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice.Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia.

stories.headline: Water cannon deployed at India education system protests
stories.summary: Indian police deployed water cannons in Raipur at student protests demanding the resignation of the education minister.

stories.headline: Death toll from blast at Indian hydroelectric project rises to 20 people
stories.summary: Rescuers say flooding and toxic gas in the collapsed tunnel make it unlikely any remaining trapped workers survived.

stories.headline: EU gives conditional green light to Paramount's Warner Bros. Discovery deal
stories.summary: Paramount's bid still faces hurdles across the Atlantic after a federal judge in California ordered the companies to temporarily pause their merger this week.

stories.headline: Hungary's prosecutor resigns under pressure in political win for Magyar
stories.summary: Hungary's Prosecutor General, Gábor Bálint Nagy, has resigned amid mounting political pressure as Prime Minister Péter Magyar seeks to replace Orbán-era officials. The move follows allegations of political bias by lawyers for detained Ukrainian cash couriers in a cross-border cash seizure case.

stories.headline: French environment minister convinced to stay on after announcing resignation
stories.summary: Barbut had resigned earlier on Wednesday and criticized the government in a LinkedIn post, saying it had reneged on promises over an agricultural bill to reintroduce certain pesticides.

stories.headline: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
stories.summary: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

stories.headline: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
stories.summary: Officials say the restaurant in South Korea used some 49,000 ants in various dishes over four years.

stories.headline: Tesla profits plunge as discounts on EV models weigh on results
stories.summary: Capital expenditures more than doubled as company accelerates pivot to semiconductors, autonomous taxis and humanoid robots

stories.headline: Burnham can be the failure that Britain needs
stories.summary: Big government must be tested to destruction for serious reform to finally happen

stories.headline: Mercedes risks US sales ban under Senate China bill

stories.headline: Vitamin Well Raises $2.2 Billion From Cross-Currency Loan Deal
stories.summary: Vitamin Well AB raised about $2.2 billion through a cross-currency loan offering to fund its acquisition of protein-bar maker EMPWR Nutrition Group.

stories.headline: GE Vernova Falls as Outlook Clashes With ‘Too High’ AI Hopes
stories.summary: GE Vernova Inc.’s shares slumped after a higher revenue outlook failed to impress investors, a sign that it’s getting harder for companies that sell equipment to data centers to meet the market’s sky-high hopes.

stories.headline: Pool Supplier Leslie’s Considers Potential Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
stories.summary: Pool supplier Leslie’s Inc. is considering a range of strategic options to address its debt load, including a potential Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Brazil Unveils New Relief for Companies Hit by US Tariffs
stories.summary: Brazil’s government unveiled a relief package worth 18.5 billion reais ($3.7 billion) in credit for exporters hit by new US tariffs, as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva seeks to shield key sectors from damage ahead of October elections.

stories.headline: Galaxy Digital Seeks $3.5 Billion Debt for Texas Data Center
stories.summary: Galaxy Digital Inc. is planning to raise about $3.5 billion from a debut junk-bond sale to fund a data center tied to CoreWeave Inc., extending AI borrowing’s push into the riskier corners of the US credit market.

stories.headline: Paulson-Backed NovaGold Strikes Deal for Full Ownership of Alaska Mine
stories.summary: NovaGold Resources Inc., a company backed by hedge-fund billionaire John Paulson, struck a deal to obtain full ownership of the Donlin Gold project in Alaska in a move to control one of the largest undeveloped gold mines.

stories.headline: Vale Elects Ollie Chairman After Weeks of Governance Turmoil
stories.summary: Vale SA shareholders elected Manuel Lino Silva de Sousa Oliveira as chairman on Wednesday, concluding weeks of boardroom turmoil sparked by an effort from one of the Brazilian mining giant’s largest investors to reshape the company’s leadership.

stories.headline: House Republicans Pass Stock Trading Bill Tied to SAVE Act and Voter ID Measure
stories.summary: The measure has drawn bipartisan backing amid a public outcry against the practice, but Democrats opposed it after Republicans added a voter ID requirement.

stories.summary: China’s foreign ministry says ‘relevant countries’ should ‘stop stirring up tension’. stories.headline: India’s Rahul Gandhi speaks out after release
stories.summary: Indian opposition leader Rahul Gandhi has spoken out publicly for the first time since he was arrested during protests.

stories.headline: UN rights chief condemns Nicaragua’s decision to abolish elections
stories.summary: UN rights chief condemns worsening repression in Nicaragua, urging the restoration of freedoms and the rule of law.

stories.summary: Anonymous donors are using food delivery apps to send food to student protesters in New Delhi

stories.summary: Protesters shouted ‘Justice for Lorenzo’ at a Houston, Texas, city council meeting on Tuesday after an ICE shooting.

stories.summary: Wall Street traders sent stocks wavering in late hours as they sifted through results from megacaps for clues on whether their massive artificial-intelligence investments are paying off.

stories.headline: Yen Rebound Ebbs After Brief Rally on Outlook for BOJ Rate Hikes
stories.summary: The yen slid beyond 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground in choppy trading on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

stories.summary: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

stories.headline: Moment fans in Madrid celebrate World Cup win
stories.summary: Thousands of spectators watched Spain's victory in central Madrid on late Sunday night.

stories.headline: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.headline: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer

stories.headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'

stories.headline: As heatwaves strike, Europeans turn to Chinese air-conditioners
